PR surface: Source +56, Tests +155. Total +211 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main lets command-authorized native Codex controls and bound inbound claims reach app-server execution paths without a current owner/admin recheck. I did not establish a live gateway/Codex runtime reproduction.

target.title: fix(codex): require admin for native controls
target.body:
## Summary

### What Problem This Solves

Sensitive native Codex controls did not consistently consume the owner/admin authorization facts already available to the command and bound-conversation paths.

### Why This Change Was Made

Native execution and binding mutation should follow the existing privileged-control boundary while read-only status, help, and invalid-usage responses remain available.

## Changes

- Require current owner identity or `operator.admin` scope for native Codex execution, control, binding, and detach operations.
- Recheck current sender authority before every bound Codex turn.
- Carry current owner and gateway-scope facts through the generic inbound-claim event.
- Add regression coverage for denied side effects, gateway-scope precedence, owner fallback, and read-only status access.

## Validation

### Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` — 3 files, 378 tests passed.
- `node scripts/check-src-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json` — no violations.
- `node scripts/check-sdk-package-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json` — no violations.
- `node scripts/check-test-helper-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json` — no violations.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/command-handlers.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts` — passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` — passed.
- Structured Codex autoreview against current upstream `main` — clean, no actionable findings.

## Notes

### User Impact

Owners and `operator.admin` callers retain native Codex control. Ordinary command-authorized senders can still use read-only/status surfaces but cannot start, mutate, detach, or drive native Codex execution.

AI-assisted change; I reviewed the implementation and validation results.
target.author: eleqtrizit
